title: Nephrodium procurrens

Here's a paragraph for Nephrolepis exaltata (Boston fern):

The Boston fern (Nephrolepis exaltata) is a species of fern native to tropical regions, characterized by its long, arching fronds. The Boston fern is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to remove air pollutants and improve indoor air quality. The bioactive constituents of the Boston fern include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the Boston fern is often prepared as a tea or extract to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Nephrodium procurrens is classified as a species of fern in the family Matoniaceae. It is a part of the genus Nephrodium, which is now considered to be a synonym of the genus Matonia. However, it is still studied by paleobotanists to gain insights into the evolution of ferns. The classification of Nephrodium procurrens is based on fossil records.
